structural_claim: Topological constraints fundamentally determine the evolution and stability of dynamical systems through conservation laws that persist except at singular structures

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Magnetic field line topology is conserved in ideal plasmas, constraining evolution to preserve topological linkage except at reconnection sites
Magnetic topology moves with the plasma fluid, establishing that topological structures are dynamically conserved
Global topological properties of band structures create protected edge states that are immune to local perturbations
